items.title: Why Do Humanoid Robots Still Struggle With the Small Stuff?
items.author: John Pavlus (Quanta Magazine)
items.summary: 人形机器人在大动作上进步巨大，但精细操作仍然困难。核心问题不是AI不够聪明，而是还没掌握力的物理学。
highlights: To have robots which work like humans, I think we have to master physics.
Force as a first-class citizen is absolutely required.
We're in the Volta stage — using AI to make robots work before we understand how it works.
items.thoughts: Position tells you WHERE to go. Force tells you HOW to touch. LLMs mastered the former; the latter remains unsolved.

items.title: 论清代边疆治理
items.author: 吕文利
items.summary: 《礼记》的「修其教不易其俗，齐其政不易其宜」不只是因俗而治，更是因俗而治+修教齐政的两阶段治理逻辑
highlights: 因俗而治是起点，修教齐政是目的
康熙帝：修德安民，喀尔喀即长城
items.thoughts: 妥协是智慧，改革也是智慧；关键在于知道什么时候该妥协，什么时候该改革

items.title: Where Some See Strings, She Sees a Space-Time Made of Fractals
items.author: Charlie Wood (采访 Astrid Eichhorn)
items.summary: Quanta专访物理学家Astrid Eichhorn，探讨渐近安全理论如何用分形结构取代弦论和圈量子引力来解决量子引力问题
highlights: OMG图：定点存在时，top和bottom夸克必须有不同质量
最保守的量子引力：不改变粒子或时空，只添加尺度对称
谦逊：弦论和圈量子引力可能是同一物理的不同视角
items.thoughts: 理论的价值在于可被证伪，而非不可动摇

items.title: Nth Country Experiment: 三个学生如何设计出原子弹
items.author: Jake Currie (Nautilus)
items.summary: 1964年五角大楼的绝密实验：三个不懂核物理的博士后从零设计原子弹，三年后成功。证明核扩散的真正壁垒不是知识，而是材料。
highlights: The big advantage we had over Fermi and Teller was that we knew it could work.
If it had been constructed, it would have made a pretty impressive bang — on the same order of magnitude as Hiroshima.
items.thoughts: 知识民主化是双刃剑。Atoms for Peace 的信息公开帮助了和平核能，也帮助了这三个学生。
